title: Lean: T19 Strict-Grammar i-Barrier
proof_strategy: Encode strict grammar at TYPE LEVEL: StrictTree.eval : StrictTree → Option ℝ. The return type is ℝ, not ℂ. T19 becomes trivial: all values are real by type. i_not_real proves i ∉ ℝ. Combination gives T19.
key_insight: The strict grammar's domain restriction (y > 0) naturally types the evaluation as ℝ-valued. This makes T19 a type-level theorem in Lean 4.
